name: AOD-9604
subtitle: Modified hGH Fragment | Fat Loss Peptide

modifications: Disulfide bond between Cys183 and Cys189

overview: AOD-9604 is a modified fragment of human growth hormone (amino acids 176-191) that stimulates lipolysis and inhibits lipogenesis without the side effects of full growth hormone. Unlike full GH, it does not increase IGF-1, affect glucose metabolism, or cause insulin resistance.
mechanism: Stimulates lipolysis and inhibits lipogenesis through beta-3 adrenergic receptor upregulation, without binding to the GH receptor. Does not affect glucose metabolism or increase IGF-1 levels.
keyBenefits: Targets fat loss through selective lipolysis
No effect on blood glucose or insulin

items.name: Lipolysis Enhancement
items.description: Increases fat oxidation, plasma glycerol levels, and reduces body weight in obese models without affecting glucose metabolism.

items.name: Lipogenesis Inhibition
items.description: Reduces fat synthesis and storage in adipose tissue.

items.name: Metabolic Safety
items.description: Does not cause hyperglycemia, reduce insulin secretion, or increase insulin resistance.

items.name: Cartilage Repair
items.description: Enhanced cartilage regeneration when combined with hyaluronic acid in osteoarthritis models.

items.name: Osteoarthritis Support
items.description: Synergistic effects with hyaluronic acid for joint health.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection is the standard route. Administer in the morning on an empty stomach, wait 30 minutes before eating for optimal results.

steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature (15-20 minutes)
Clean vial stopper with alcohol swab
Inject slowly down vial side (not directly onto powder)
Gently swirl until dissolved (never shake vigorously)

keyBenefits: Abdomen (near navel) or thigh injection sites

interactions.notes: Enhanced cartilage repair when combined for joint health.
Different mechanisms; sometimes combined for comprehensive healing.
AOD-9604 doesn't affect GH axis; can be used together.
Complementary weight loss mechanisms through different pathways.
AOD-9604 doesn't affect glucose metabolism unlike full hGH.

timeline.effects: Subtle body composition changes may begin
More noticeable fat loss, especially with diet and exercise
Continued fat reduction and improved body composition

common: No reported effects on blood glucose or insulin
stopSigns: Severe injection site reactions or infection signs
contraindications: WADA prohibited - athletes subject to testing must avoid

good: White lyophilized powder with uniform appearance
Crystal clear solution without particles after reconstitution
Certificate of Analysis showing >98% purity by HPLC
warning: Not FDA approved as a therapeutic drug - research chemical only
bad: Discolored or clumped powder (yellow indicates degradation)

references.title: Increase of fat oxidation and weight loss in obese mice caused by chronic treatment with human growth hormone or a modified C-terminal fragment
references.authors: Heffernan MA, Thorburn AW, Fam B, Summers R, Conway-Campbell B, Waters MJ, Ng FM
references.keyFindings: Both hGH and AOD-9604 reduced body weight gain, increased fat oxidation, and stimulated lipolysis in obese ob/ob mice after 14 days chronic IP administration, without affecting glucose metabolism.

references.title: The effects of human GH and its lipolytic fragment (AOD9604) on lipid metabolism following chronic treatment in obese mice and beta(3)-AR knock-out mice
references.authors: Heffernan MA, Jiang WJ, Thorburn AW, Ng FM
references.keyFindings: Both hGH and AOD-9604 increased repressed beta-3 adrenergic receptor RNA expression in obese mice to levels comparable with lean mice. Effects abolished in beta-3-AR knockout mice, confirming pathway involvement.

references.title: Effects of oral administration of a synthetic fragment of human growth hormone on lipid metabolism
references.authors: Heffernan MA, Heffernan MJ, Jiang WJ, Thorburn AW, Ng FM
references.journal: American Journal of Physiology - Endocrinology and Metabolism
references.keyFindings: Oral treatment of ob/ob mice with AOD-9401 for 30 days significantly reduced body weight gain and altered lipogenic and lipolytic activity in adipose tissue.

references.title: Metabolic studies of a synthetic lipolytic domain (AOD9604) of human growth hormone
references.authors: Ng FM, Jiang WJ, Gianello R, Pitt C, Heffernan M
references.keyFindings: Characterized metabolic properties of AOD-9604 as a synthetic lipolytic domain of hGH with antilipogenic activity that does not interact with the GH receptor.

references.title: Effect of Intra-articular Injection of AOD9604 with or without Hyaluronic Acid in Rabbit Osteoarthritis Model
references.authors: Kwon DR, Park GY, Lee SU
references.journal: Annals of Clinical and Laboratory Science
references.keyFindings: Intra-articular AOD-9604 injections enhanced cartilage regeneration in collagenase-induced osteoarthritis rabbit model. Combined AOD-9604 and hyaluronic acid injections were more effective than either alone.

latestResearch.title: Detection and in vitro metabolism of AOD9604
latestResearch.summary: Characterized in vitro metabolism and developed detection methods for AOD-9604 as a WADA-prohibited substance, relevant for anti-doping analysis.

quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: room temp or freezer long-term. Reconstituted: 2-8°C for 28 days
